question_text: Let Xi, i = 1, 2, . . . , n, be i.i.d. random variables from a normal distribution with mean 1 and variance 4. Let Sn = X2 1 + X2 2 + · · · + X2 n. If Var(Sn) denotes the variance of Sn, then the value of lim n→∞ Var(Sn) E(Sn) 2! (in integer) is equal to

Quick Tip
When dealing with sums of squares of normal random variables, use properties of vari-
ance and expectation, along with the fact that the variance of a chi-squared distribution
is related to the number of degrees of freedom.
